latest.title: Stabilisation Notice-TotalEnergies SE
latest.content:
19 Feb 2026 15:55
RNS Number : 7237T
Societe Generale SA
19 February 2026
19th February 2026
Not for distribution, directly or indirectly, in or into the United States or any jurisdiction in which such distribution would be unlawful.
TotalEnergies SE (Ticker: TTEFP, Country: FR))
Pre-stabilisation Period Announcement
Societe Generale (contact: Nicole Bezzant +44 207 676 8656) hereby gives notice, as Stabilisation Coordinator, that the Stabilisation Manager(s) named below may stabilise the offer of the following securities in accordance with [Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 under the Market Abuse Regulation (EU/596/2014)][and] [the UK FCA Stabilisation Binding Technical Standards].
Securities
Issuer:
TotalEnergies SE
Guarantor(s) (if any):
N/A
Aggregate nominal amount:
EUR 1.5bn
Description:
Coupon: 3.790% Coupon Fixed rate, payable annually in arrear until First Reset Date; then resets on First Reset Date and every 5 years at € 5 year Mid-swaps + Initial Margin + step ups (when applicable) (subject to benchmark discontinuation provisions)
Maturity: Undated Non-call 5.25 Year
Offer price:
99.966
Stabilisation:
Stabilisation Manager(s)
SG CIB
Stabilisation period expected to start on
19/02/2026
Stabilisation period expected to end no later than
27/02/2026
Existence, maximum size and conditions of use of over
‑
allotment facility:
The Stabilisation Manager(s) may over
‑
allot the securities to the extent permitted in accordance with applicable law.
In connection with the offer of the above securities, the Stabilisation Manager(s) may over
‑
allot10 the securities or effect transactions with a view to supporting the market price of the securities during the stabilisation period at a level higher than that which might otherwise prevail. However, stabilisation may not necessarily occur and any stabilisation action, if begun, may cease at any time11. Any stabilisation action or over
‑
allotment shall be conducted in accordance with all applicable laws and rules.
This announcement is for information purposes only and does not constitute an invitation or offer to underwrite, subscribe for or otherwise acquire or dispose of any securities of the Issuer in any jurisdiction.
This announcement and the offer of the securities to which it relates are only addressed to and directed at persons outside the United Kingdom and persons in the United Kingdom who have professional experience in matters related to investments or who are high net worth persons within Article 12(5) of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Financial Promotion) Order 2005 and must not be acted on or relied on by other persons in the United Kingdom12.
In addition, if and to the extent that this announcement is communicated in, or the offer of the securities to which it relates is made in, [the UK] [any EEA Member State] before the publication of a prospectus in relation to the securities which has been approved by the competent authority in [the UK] [that Member State] in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2017/1129 [as it forms part of domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 201813] (the "[
EU
][
UK
]
Prospectus Regulation
") [(or which has been approved by a competent authority in another Member State and notified to the competent authority that Member State in accordance with the EU Prospectus Regulation)], this announcement and the offer are only addressed to and directed at persons in [the UK] [that Member State] who are qualified investors within the meaning of the [EU][UK] Prospectus Regulation (or who are other persons to whom the offer may lawfully be addressed) and must not be acted on or relied on by other persons in [the UK] [that Member State]14.
This announcement is not an offer of securities for sale into the United States. The securities have not been, and will not be, registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933 and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an exemption from registration. There will be no public offer of securities in the United States.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our

.
END

company_30s.what_they_do: TotalEnergies is a global energy group that produces oil, gas and renewable power.  It runs refineries, distributes fuels, and now builds solar farms, wind projects and electric‑vehicle charging stations.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company is a key player in the transition to cleaner energy.  Its renewables arm is growing fast and it has contracts that lock in power for major tech and aviation customers, giving it a steady cash flow that can support future expansion.
company_30s.current_state: TotalEnergies is still a large, diversified energy firm.  It has recently added several renewable deals and is working on solar projects for data‑center operators, but its share price has slid sharply from recent highs.

rns_narrative: TotalEnergies has been signing a string of renewable‑energy contracts, especially solar farms for data‑center operators and large industrial customers.  These deals are long‑term and bring in reliable revenue.
The company is also expanding its exploration portfolio in Africa and the Middle East, adding new licences that could boost future oil and gas output.
On the regulatory side, TotalEnergies has secured approvals for several renewable projects in Europe, which could reduce its carbon footprint and improve its ESG profile.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Maximum silence – almost no one is talking about the stock right now.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bullish chatter – the market is ignoring the company.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish voices are almost absent, signalling a classic capitulation phase where the market has forgotten the stock.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has moved from mild uncertainty to complete apathy; the stock is in a deep, quiet trough.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows a flat, dead trend line – the public has no interest, so a surprise catalyst could create a big move.

near_term_catalysts: A first‑time solar‑farm contract win that locks in a 15‑year supply for a major tech company.
A positive earnings update that highlights higher renewable revenue and better margins.
A volume spike on the daily chart that shows institutional buying after a period of thin trading.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a daily close that breaks above the recent low while volume climbs above the 5‑month average.  Also keep an eye on short‑interest data – a sudden drop could signal covering.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price is likely in a late‑stage washout.  Contrarians who act now are buying when the market has already priced in the worst case.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Contrarian Bullish – the deep crash and lack of attention create a high‑reward opportunity.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen far below its long‑term support and the market has stopped watching it.  A renewable contract or earnings beat can trigger a quick reversal, and the compressed price range means a 50% rebound would double the current value.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The only downside is a modest further decline if the company misses a key contract or faces regulatory hurdles.  Even then the stock would still be above the 70‑year low, so the risk is limited.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk 20% downside versus potential 100% upside – a 1:5 asymmetry that is very attractive for a contrarian.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter 25% of the target position at the current low, then add another 25% if the daily close stays above the low and volume rises.  Place a tight stop 5% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a daily close above the low with volume above the 5‑month average, or until a new renewable contract is announced.  Do not trade on rumors alone.
playbook.if_passing: Skip this stock if you see any signs of a regulatory ban, forced divestment, or a credible takeover threat that could wipe out the remaining upside.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep, silent trough with renewable growth catalysts – a classic contrarian play that offers a 1:5 risk/reward.
